strengths: Established proper traction with Maryland forceps
Maintained steady cutting motion through most of the circle
Demonstrated good instrument coordination during the cutting phase
weaknesses: Failed to complete the full circle cut
Did not free the marked circle from the gauze
Lost momentum in the final portion of the task

drills.name: Complete circle tracking drill
drills.description: Cut practice circles in four controlled quadrants, ensuring each quadrant connects smoothly to the next without stopping until the circle is fully freed.
drills.targets_weakness: Failed to complete the full circle cut

drills.name: Circle liberation check drill
drills.description: After cutting each practice circle, verify the piece can be lifted free from the gauze before considering the task complete.
drills.targets_weakness: Did not free the marked circle from the gauze

drills.name: Sustained cutting rhythm practice
drills.description: Practice maintaining consistent cutting speed and pressure throughout the entire circle without pausing or slowing in the final quarter.
drills.targets_weakness: Lost momentum in the final portion of the task

progression_note: At the intermediate level, task completion is non-negotiable - focus on finishing the full circle cut before working on speed optimization. Your cutting technique shows promise but needs to be sustained through the entire circumference.
